story.title: The robotaxi law that could ban Tesla
story.summary: For more than a decade, one question has loomed over the race to build autonomous vehicles: Are cameras alone enough to safely replace human drivers, or do truly driverless cars need additional, overlapping sensors like lidar and radar to navigate the world reliably? Tesla has bet billions of dollars that artificial intelligence and cameras are […]

points.heading: New Jersey's Robotaxi Rule
points.detail: A bill would require companies to use cameras plus two other sensing technologies, like lidar and radar.

points.heading: Tesla's Robotaxis Banned?
points.detail: The measure would effectively prevent Tesla’s camera-only Robotaxi system from operating in New Jersey unless hardware changes.

points.heading: Prioritizing NJ Safety
points.detail: Bill sponsor Andrew Zwicker believes autonomous vehicles should roll out cautiously for New Jersey safety.

points.heading: Industry Sensor Debate
points.detail: Tesla bets cameras alone are sufficient; nearly every other major AV developer uses additional sensors like lidar and radar.
